figures.caption: Figure 6. Chemical potential µ0 versus filling n in the unstable regime of the A-site (Wyckoff 4f) altermagnetic phase at V = 0. The region with negative slope ∂µ0/∂n < 0 signals negative compressibility and a tendency toward macroscopic phase separation. The dashed line indicates the Maxwell construction used to determine the coexistence window.

Figure 4. Non-relativistic lifting of Kramers degeneracy for U = 5: (a) (t, t′, t′′) = (1, 0.5, 0) and (b) (t, t′, t′′) = (1, 0.5, 0.5). The dashed square indicates the boundary of the first Brillouin zone.

Figure 3. (a) Illustration of the tight-binding hopping parameters: t (A-B hopping), t′ (intra-unit-cell A-A hopping), and t′′ (inter-unit-cell A-A hopping). (b) and (c) show the Fermi surfaces for (t, t′, t′′) = (1, 0.5, 0) and (t, t′, t′′) = (1, 0.5, 0.5) at fillings nf ≈ 0.35 and nf ≈ 0.68, respectively. The corresponding band dispersions are presented in (d) and (e), where the red line indicates the Fermi level (filling). The density of states (DOS) associated with each dispersion is shown on the right. For these two fillings, (f) and (g) display the sublattice weights along the paths M → M1 → M2 → M3 → M and Γ → M3 → Γ′ → M → Γ, respectively.
